# Explore Mode Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Build a public Explore page with 30 curated destination guides, category filtering, rich detail pages, and "Plan a trip" CTAs that convert browsers into bookers.

**Architecture:** Fully static frontend feature. A `destinations.ts` data file contains curated content for 30 cities (generated by Claude, reviewed by human). The Explore page (`/explore`) renders a filterable card grid. Each destination has a detail page (`/explore/[slug]`) with experiences, dining, neighborhoods, weather, and advisories. Next.js statically generates all pages at build time for SEO.

**Tech Stack:** Next.js 15 (App Router, static generation), SCSS modules, existing Unsplash image system, Mediterranean Warmth palette

**Design Spec:** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-03-explore-mode-design.md`

**Verification before every commit:** `pnpm format:check && pnpm lint && pnpm test && pnpm build`

---

## File Structure

### New Files

```
web-client/src/data/destinations.ts                    # Static content for 30 cities
web-client/src/app/explore/page.tsx                    # Explore page (hero + filters + grid)
web-client/src/app/explore/page.module.scss            # Explore page styles
web-client/src/app/explore/[slug]/page.tsx             # Destination detail page
web-client/src/app/explore/[slug]/page.module.scss     # Detail page styles
```

### Modified Files

```
web-client/src/components/Header/Header.tsx            # Add "Explore" nav link
web-client/src/app/page.tsx                            # Add "Explore destinations" CTA
```

---

## Task 1: Destination Content Data File

**Files:**

- Create: `web-client/src/data/destinations.ts`

This is the largest task — generating curated content for 30 cities. The file exports the `Destination` interface and a `DESTINATIONS` array.

- [ ] **Step 1: Create the data file with types and content**

Create `web-client/src/data/destinations.ts` with:

1. The `Destination` interface (from spec)
2. The `CATEGORY_FILTERS` constant for the filter bar
3. The `DESTINATIONS` array with all 30 cities
4. A `getDestinationBySlug(slug: string)` helper

Each city entry needs:

- `slug`: URL-friendly (`new-york`, `cape-town`)
- `name`: display name
- `country`: full country name
- `categories`: array of tags from `beach`, `city`, `adventure`, `romantic`, `food-wine`, `culture`, `budget`, `family`
- `price_level`: 1-4 ($-$$$$)
- `best_season`: e.g., "March - May"
- `description`: 2-3 paragraphs of engaging travel copy
- `currency`, `language`: local info
- `estimated_daily_budget`: `{ budget, mid, luxury }` in USD
- `visa_summary`: one-line visa info for US travelers
- `top_experiences`: 10 items with name, category, description, estimated_cost
- `dining_highlights`: 4-6 restaurants with name, cuisine, price_level, description
- `neighborhoods`: 3-4 areas with name + character description
- `weather`: 12 months with high_c, low_c, rainfall_mm

**Generate this content using Claude.** For each of the 30 cities, Claude writes the content based on real travel knowledge. The content should be accurate, engaging, and informative — not generic AI filler.

The 30 cities: Tokyo, Paris, New York, London, Barcelona, Rome, Santorini, Bali, Sydney, Dubai, Singapore, Seoul, Lisbon, Istanbul, Bangkok, Kyoto, Cape Town, Marrakech, Havana, Rio de Janeiro, Amsterdam, Prague, Vienna, Budapest, Dubrovnik, Reykjavik, Cusco, Naples, Maldives, Amman

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify the file compiles**

Run: `pnpm format:check && pnpm build`
Expected: Build passes (the data file is just types + a const array)

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git commit -m "feat: add curated destination content for 30 cities"
```

---

## Task 2: Explore Page — Hero + Filters + Card Grid

**Files:**

- Create: `web-client/src/app/explore/page.tsx`
- Create: `web-client/src/app/explore/page.module.scss`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create the Explore page**

The page is a **server component** (no `'use client'` — filtering uses a client component wrapper).

Actually, since filtering requires `useState`, this needs to be a client component. Use `'use client'`.

```tsx
'use client';

import { useState } from 'react';

import {
  CATEGORY_FILTERS,
  DESTINATIONS,
  type Destination,
} from '@/data/destinations';
import {
  HERO_IMAGES,
  getDestinationImage,
  getDestinationImageUrl,
} from '@/lib/destinationImage';
import Image from 'next/image';
import Link from 'next/link';

import styles from './page.module.scss';
```

Layout:

1. Hero section — reuse `HERO_IMAGES` from destinationImage.ts with rotating carousel (same pattern as home page hero)
2. Category filter bar — row of pill buttons. `activeCategory` state, default "all". Clicking a category filters the grid.
3. Destination card grid — map over filtered `DESTINATIONS`, render cards with Image + info

Each card:

```tsx
<Link href={`/explore/${dest.slug}`} className={styles.card}>
  <div className={styles.cardImage}>
    {imageUrl ? (
      <Image
        src={imageUrl}
        alt={dest.name}
        fill
        sizes='(max-width: 600px) 100vw, (max-width: 900px) 50vw, 33vw'
        style={{ objectFit: 'cover' }}
      />
    ) : (
      <div className={styles.cardImageFallback}>{dest.name}</div>
    )}
  </div>
  <div className={styles.cardContent}>
    <h3>{dest.name}</h3>
    <p className={styles.country}>{dest.country}</p>
    <div className={styles.cardMeta}>
      <span className={styles.price}>{'$'.repeat(dest.price_level)}</span>
      <span className={styles.season}>Best: {dest.best_season}</span>
    </div>
    <div className={styles.tags}>
      {dest.categories.slice(0, 2).map((cat) => (
        <span key={cat} className={styles.tag}>
          {formatCategory(cat)}
        </span>
      ))}
    </div>
  </div>
</Link>
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Style the Explore page**

`page.module.scss` using Mediterranean Warmth palette:

- `.hero`: same pattern as home page (rotating images, overlay, text)
- `.filters`: flex row of pill buttons, horizontal scroll on mobile, gap 8px
- `.filterPill`: border radius 20px, padding 8px 16px, border 1px solid var(--border). Active: var(--cta) background, var(--cta-text) color
- `.grid`: CSS grid, `grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr)`, gap 24px. 2 columns at 900px, 1 at 600px
- `.card`: border-radius var(--radius-md), overflow hidden, border, hover shadow+transform
- `.cardImage`: height 200px, relative, overflow hidden
- `.cardContent`: padding 16px
- `.price`: font-weight 600, var(--accent)
- `.season`: var(--foreground-muted), font-size 13px
- `.tags`: flex row of small pill badges

- [ ] **Step 3: Verify**

Run: `pnpm format:check && pnpm lint && pnpm test && pnpm build`

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git commit -m "feat: add Explore page with hero carousel, category filters, and destination card grid"
```

## Task 3: Destination Detail Page

**Files:**

- Create: `web-client/src/app/explore/[slug]/page.tsx`
- Create: `web-client/src/app/explore/[slug]/page.module.scss`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create the detail page with static generation**

This is a **server component** — no client-side state needed. Use Next.js `generateStaticParams` for static generation + `generateMetadata` for SEO.

```tsx
import { DESTINATIONS, getDestinationBySlug } from '@/data/destinations';
import {
  getDestinationImage,
  getDestinationImageUrl,
} from '@/lib/destinationImage';
import Image from 'next/image';
import Link from 'next/link';
import { notFound } from 'next/navigation';

import styles from './page.module.scss';

export function generateStaticParams() {
  return DESTINATIONS.map((d) => ({ slug: d.slug }));
}

export function generateMetadata({ params }: { params: { slug: string } }) {
  const dest = getDestinationBySlug(params.slug);
  if (!dest) return {};
  return {
    title: `${dest.name} Travel Guide — Voyager`,
    description: dest.description.split('.')[0] + '.',
    openGraph: {
      title: `${dest.name} Travel Guide — Voyager`,
      description: dest.description.split('.')[0] + '.',
    },
  };
}
```

Page layout renders all 9 sections from the spec:

1. Hero image (300px, full-width, city + country overlaid)
2. Quick stats bar (currency, language, best season, daily budget, visa)
3. About (description rendered as paragraphs)
4. Top 10 Experiences (card grid)
5. Dining Highlights (card list)
6. Neighborhoods (simple list with descriptions)
7. Weather (12-month table or bar chart)
8. Travel Advisories (visa_summary displayed)
9. CTA ("Plan a trip to [city]" button)

The CTA button links to `/trips/new?destination=[city-name]` (the new trip page can pre-fill the destination). No auth check needed on this page — the trips/new page handles auth.

- [ ] **Step 2: Style the detail page**

`page.module.scss`:

- `.hero`: 300px, relative, full-width, gradient overlay, white text
- `.statsBar`: flex row, gap, border-bottom, padding
- `.stat`: flex column, label (12px muted) + value (14px bold)
- `.section`: padding 40px 0, max-width 1000px margin auto
- `.sectionTitle`: Fraunces 28px, margin-bottom 24px
- `.experienceGrid`: 2-column grid (1 on mobile)
- `.experienceCard`: surface bg, border, padding, border-radius
- `.diningList`: vertical stack with dividers
- `.neighborhoodList`: vertical stack
- `.weatherTable`: table or flex row of month columns
- `.ctaSection`: centered, padding 60px, background surface-alt
- `.ctaButton`: coral CTA, large padding, Fraunces font

- [ ] **Step 3: Verify**

Run: `pnpm format:check && pnpm lint && pnpm test && pnpm build`
Expected: Build generates static pages for all 30 slugs

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git commit -m "feat: add destination detail pages with experiences, dining, weather, and CTA"
```

## Task 4: Header Nav + Home Page CTA

**Files:**

- Modify: `web-client/src/components/Header/Header.tsx`
- Modify: `web-client/src/app/page.tsx`

- [ ] **Step 1: Add Explore to both public and authed nav links**

In `Header.tsx`, add `{ href: '/explore', label: 'Explore' }` to BOTH `publicLinks` and `authedLinks` arrays. Position it first (before FAQ for public, before My Trips for authed).

- [ ] **Step 2: Add Explore CTA to home page**

In `page.tsx`, add an "Explore destinations" section between the features section and the demo section. A simple link styled as a secondary CTA:

```tsx
<section className={styles.exploreSection}>
  <h2>Explore Destinations</h2>
  <p>
    Browse 30 curated travel guides with local tips, dining recommendations, and
    insider knowledge.
  </p>
  <Link href='/explore' className={styles.exploreCta}>
    Discover destinations
  </Link>
</section>
```

Add styles for `.exploreSection` and `.exploreCta` in `page.module.scss`.

- [ ] **Step 3: Verify**

Run: `pnpm format:check && pnpm lint && pnpm test && pnpm build`

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git commit -m "feat: add Explore nav link and home page CTA"
```

No Railway deploy needed — this is purely frontend. No database changes.

## Self-Review

**Spec coverage:**

- ✅ Public Explore page with hero, filters, grid (Task 2)
- ✅ Category filtering — client-side on static data (Task 2)
- ✅ 30 curated destinations with full content (Task 1)
- ✅ Destination detail pages with all 9 sections (Task 3)
- ✅ SEO — generateStaticParams + generateMetadata (Task 3)
- ✅ "Plan a trip" CTA with auth-aware behavior (Task 3)
- ✅ Header nav link for public + authed users (Task 4)
- ✅ Home page CTA (Task 4)
- ✅ Unsplash images reused via getDestinationImage (Tasks 2, 3)
- ✅ Mediterranean Warmth palette (Tasks 2, 3)

**Placeholder scan:** Task 1 describes content generation — the implementing agent must actually generate full content for 30 cities, not placeholder data. This is the most time-intensive task.

**Type consistency:** `Destination` interface defined in Task 1, used in Tasks 2-3. `getDestinationBySlug` defined in Task 1, used in Task 3. `CATEGORY_FILTERS` defined in Task 1, used in Task 2. All consistent.
